ety: inf. n. ii of ملك 'to possess'
def: s.f. Appointing master or possessor; possessorship; possession

ety: part. n. of تمّ 'to become complete'
def: adj. Complete, entire, whole, full; perfect, consummate.
more: (part. act. of تمام) adj. Perfect, entire, complete, full.

def: adj. Very covetous, avaricious, wishful, extremely greedy.
more: adj. (superl. of طمع) Covetous, avaricious, wishful, greedy.

def: (3rd. pers. sing. perf. of تمّ), It is completed, is finished, is complete
ex: کیا تم مجھ سے محبت کرتی ہو؟
kyā tum mujh-se mohabbat kartī ho?
تم جیسے لوگوں کی ہی ضرورت ہے یہاں۔
tum jaise logõ kī hī zarūrat hai yahā̃.
People like you are needed here.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुम्हि (tumhi), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (tumhe), from Sanskrit युष्मान् (yuṣmān, acc. pl.) adopting the initial sounds of त्वम् (tvám, nom. sg.).

def: (3rd pers. sing. fem. of the perf. of تمّ); See tamma.

s.m. See s.v. tamas for compounds of which the first member is tamo. adj. & s.m. Dark; affected by or appertaining to the third quality or that of darkness (vice, ignorance, &c.); inert; stupid, ignorant; vicious, vindictive; irascible; malignant; — a malignant or mischievous person, a villain, &c.
s.m. A ripe date, a date
more: s. m. A date. تمرهندي tamr-i-hindī, s. f. A tamarind.

def: adj. & s.m. Of a coppery red colour; — coppery, consisting of copper; — copper; a dark or coppery red; — a kind of sandal-wood (said to be of a dark red colour and to smell like the lotus)
subs: s.m. A copper plate (on which Hindū grants of land, &c. are frequently inscribed)
s.m. A copper-coloured or red flower; the plant Bauhinia variegata, or a kind of ebony; — the plant Kæmpferia rotunda
s.m. A red kind of sandal, Pterospermum santolinum
s.f. Name of the female elephant of the western quarter
s.m. Sulphate of copper, blue vitriol
s.m. A red sort of sandal wood; the plant Dolichos uniflorus
s.t. The China rose; the blossom of sesamum
s.f. The plant Rubia mungista, Bengal madder
s.m. A kind of vetch, Dolichos uniflorus.

def: s.m. = tāṅbṛā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Copper-coloured, dark red (e.g. tāṅbṛī maṭṭī, 'red earth'); — copper-colour (in pigeons, &c.); a copper-coloured gem of inferior value; a false stone resembling a ruby; — a bald head.
more: s. m. A gem of inferiour value, of a copper colour.

def: n. m. From H. 1. A paste stone resembling a ruby. 2. A copper color (in pigeons). 3. Head; skull. Tāmṛā phaṭ gayā . 4. A bald head ( ganj .)
s.m. Name of one of the eighteen divisions of the known continent.

s.f. A copper or metallic cup of prescribed capacity and perforated by a small hole at the bottom (when placed in a vessel of water it answers the purpose of a clepsydra, and the water gradually filling it marks the time that has elapsed).
adj. & s.m. Coppery, made of copper; — a coppersmith, brazier.

s.f. A coppersmith's wife; — a kind of clepsydra ( = tāmrī, q.v.). s.f. A copper or metallic cup of prescribed capacity and perforated by a small hole at the bottom (when placed in a vessel of water it answers the purpose of a clepsydra, and the water gradually filling it marks the time that has elapsed).
practice, exercise
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَمْرِين (tamrīn).

def: s.f. Likening, comparing; assimilation; comparison, similitude, resemblance, fac-simile, counterpart; example, allegory, fable, apologue; — tams̤īl lānā, v.n. To adduce examples; to employ parables, &c.; to allegorize.
more: n. f. مثل 1. A simile ( misāl ). 2. An allegory; a parable.

(inf. ii. of مثل) s. f. 1. Comparison, similitude. 2. Allegory, parable, apologue, an example, a counterpart or duplicate, a fac simile. (Plur. تمثيلات tams̤īlāt ). تمثيل لانا tams̤īl lānā, To allegorize.

def: adv. By way of similitude, allegorically, as an example.
more: adv. By way of similitude, allegorically.

def: s.f. pl. (of tams̤īl), Examples, allegories, &c.
s.f. A small timbrel or drum, a tom-tom; the sound of a drum or tom-tom.

ety: fr. tamtamānā; — ˚āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअ+ट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+त्वं
def: s.f. The state of growing red (in the face); flush, glow.
more: s. f. The state of growing red (in the face), glow.

def: n. f. Glow; flush.
more: n. f. A faint light; twinkling; twinkle.

ety: tamtam˚ = S. तम् redupl.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. increment
def: v.n. To become or grow red (in the face), to flush; to glow; to sparkle; to twinkle; to flash.
more: v. a. S. तमः To glow; to be flushed or red in the face. Bukhār se mūṅh tamtamā rahā hai .
v. a. 1. To grow red (in the face). 2. To glow, to sparkle, to twinkle, to flash.

def: s.m. The Syrian month corresponding to July O.S.

s.f. A tone; a tune; the key-note (in music); — stretching, tension; knitting (the eyebrows); — a thread; a stretched or tight cord or rope; a rigid rod or bar, a tie-rod (as the rods which give fixity to the poles of a pālkī, or the iron bar of a cart, or across a bedstead)
subs: tān toṛnā, v.n. To beat time; to strike up a tune; to strike the key-note; — to crack a joke; to drop a word which induces conversation or excites a quarrel; to animadvert
v.n. To strike up a tune; to sing or play in tune; — to poke fun (at), to crack a joke ( = ṭhaṭṭhā mārnā)
v.n. To pitch or to catch the key-note; to tune.

def: s.f. The plant Verbena (or Zepania) nodiflora.
s.m. The body, person; one's own person, self
more: n. m. S. तुन्न A tree of which the wood, resembling mahogany, is used for furniture. Cedrela toona . The flower is used for dying a yellow color.

n. m. S. तनु The body; person. 1. Tan sukhī to man sukhī . Prov. The body at ease, the mind at ease. 2. Sīna ba sīna, tan ba tan, aish o tarab ke sab baran . Nazīr. Limb pressed to limb, and breast to breast, All transports keen at once expressed. 3. Tan ko kapṛā, na peṭ ko roṭī . Prov. Nor clothing for the body, nor bread for the belly. tan-par-var , adj. 1. Self-indulgent; luxurious. 2. Selfish; self-indulgent. tan-parvarī , n. f. Self-indulgence; ease; luxuriousness. tan tanhā ; Wom. tane tanhā , adv. Singly; alone; all alone.

s. m. The body, person. تن پرور tan-parwar, Careful of the body, over attentive to the body. تن پروري tan-parwar-ī, s. f. Self-indulgence. تن تنـﮩ‍ا tan-i-tanhā, adv. Alone, solitary. تن درست tan-durust, adj. Healthy, vigorous. تندرستي tan-durust-ī, s. f. Health, vigour. تن ده tan-dih, A person of application. تندهي tan-dih-ī, s. f. Application, exertion, attention, diligence. تن دينا tan denā, To pay attention. تن ومن مارنا tan-o-man mārnā, To restrain oneʼs desires and be silent.
1. s. m. Name of a tree. 2. s. f. The flower of the tree (used for dying a yellow colour). (Cedrela toona: Roxb. ).
subs: s.f. Ease of body, bodily comfort, indulgence
adj. & s.m. Careful of the body, self-indulgent; selfish; luxurious, voluptuous; — a self-indulgent person, &c.
s.f. A pampering of the body, self-indulgence, ease, luxury, voluptuousness
adj. Well in health, sound in body, healthy, vigorous
v.t. To make (one) well, to restore to health, to cure
adj. & s.m. Applying oneself (to), attentive (to), of great application, diligent; — one who has great application, &c.
s.f. Application, attention, diligence; exertion, effort, pains
adv. Attentively, diligently; energetically, with exertion or effort, with painstaking
tan denā (-meṅ), To give or apply oneself (to), to pay attention (to), to exert oneself (in), to take pains
s.f. A cloth of the nainsukh kind, but finer; a kind of vest worn under the qabā
adj. & s.m. Taking ease, reposing, indulging; idling; bodily ease, &c. = tan-āsānī, q.v.
(-ke), To touch or affect the person (of); to come home to one, to feel; to assimilate (food), to nourish, to make flesh, to fatten; to be applied to the body, be brought into use
adj. Personally attached (to, -ke), devoted (to), zealous (for); active, energetic, painstaking
s.m. Body and soul, one's whole self
adv. With body and soul, with all (one's) heart and soul

tan-o-man mārnā, v.n. To restrain the appetites or desires; to suppress the feelings or emotions; to keep quiet or silent; to concentrate one's attention or faculties (on), to render oneself unconscious of snrrounding objects, &c.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀡𑀽 (taṇū), from Sanskrit तनू (tanū́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *tanúHs, from Proto-Indo-European *tenúh₂s, from *tenuh₂- (“body”).

def: s.m. Twang; sound (com. ṭhan)
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀡𑀽 (taṇū), from Sanskrit तनू (tanū́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *tanúHs, from Proto-Indo-European *tenúh₂s, from *tenuh₂- (“body”).

def: s.m. Haughtiness, pride, conceit, vanity.
more: s. m. 1. Twang. 2. Pride, conceit, vanity. ٿن ٿن ṭan ṭan, A sound.

ety: inf. n. of طعن 'to pierce or wound with a spear,' &c.
def: s.m. Thrusting or striking with a spear; a thrust, a stab; — blame, reproach, disapprobation, &c. ( = t̤aʻna, q.v.).
more: n. m. Taunts ( bol . 2). 1. Ochhe ke ghar khānā, janam janam kā tanā . Prov. Dine with the mean, life-long taunts keen. 2. Auraṭeṅ mujhe kahveṅgi, 'aë bhāiyoṅ pīṭī!" Yeh tanā rahī sunne ko maiṅ, hāë, Sakina! Mah. Wom. Dirge over Husain. The women all will say 'oh! mourner of thy brothers slain!' Alas! Sakīna, that such taunts your ears perforce distrain! tān toṛnā , v. a. To taunt one as though he were under an obligation to the speaker. tānā denā, yā mārnā , v. a. To taunt. tānā-zan , n. m. One who taunts another. tānā-zanī , n. f. Taunting; reproaching.
(Heb. and Ch. To load, to pierce) s. m. 1. Blame, disapprobation, reproach, chiding, censure. 2. Striking with a spear.

def: n. f. An imitative sound like 'ding-dong.' ṭanṭanā'nā, ṭunṭunānā , v. a. To ring a bell.
adv., corr. of tahāṅ, q.v.
more: n. f. S. तन to stretch. A tune; the key note in music. Ek achraj maiṅ aisā dekhā, billī chābe pān, Chūhā bajāve ḍholkī, aur meṅḍak toṛe tān . A satire on the world. Kabīr. A thing most strange I saw, a cat was chewing pān , A rat played on the drum, the frog beat time to song. (Wrong men in wrong places). tān toṛ'nā, tān uṛā'nā , v. n. To beat time. 1. Kūde, phāṅde, toṛe tān, tākā dunyā rākhe mān . Prov. Who dance and sing command the world's regard. 2. Lākh tarah pātar samjhātī, Dayā kare nahīṅ kān, Pī rahā dārū kā piyālā, uṛ rahī hai tān . Kaṛka Dayā Rām. A thousand ways his mistress counsels Dayā Rām, he heeds Her not, the wine cup still he quaffs, the revelry proceeds. rasī'lī, bhīgī, yā mīṭhī tān , n. f. Exquisite music.
n. f. 1. A plant. 2. Iron bars for supporting the poles of a palankeen; an iron bar in a native cart.

s. f. 1. A tune, the key note in musick. تان توڙنا tān toṛnā, To crack a joke, to drop a word which shall excite quarrelling or induce conversation, to animadvert: to strike up a tune. 2. (from تاننا) Stretching, tension, knitting (eyebrows).

def: s.m. The threads that are extended lengthwise upon a loom, the warp
more: n. m. S. तन् to stretch out. The warp. Tāne ghāṭ, ki bāne ghāṭ? Prov. The warp as the woof. (Both alike).
v. a. S. तापन 1. To heat; raise heat by blowing with bellows; to melt; anneal; fuse. 2. To examine; try; test.
s. m. The warp, the threads that are extended lengthwise upon a loom. تانا بانا کرنا tānā bānā karnā, To fidget, to dance attendance. تاني تنا tāne tannā, To wander here and there without profit.
(see تاونا taʼonā ) v. a. To heat, to prove, to assay.
subs: v.n. To fidget, to dance attendance
(-ke), To set or dispose the warp (of a piece of cloth); to weave; — to wander hither and thither without profit; to plan and exert oneself to no purpose.

def: v.t. To heat; to raise heat (by blowing with bellows, &c.); to anneal, to temper; to melt, fuse; to twist (by heating = tāʼo denā); to prove, test, assay.
s.m. = tānā, q.v. s.m. The threads that are extended lengthwise upon a loom, the warp
subs: v.n. To fidget, to dance attendance
(-ke), To set or dispose the warp (of a piece of cloth); to weave; — to wander hither and thither without profit; to plan and exert oneself to no purpose.

def: (corrupted from طعن تعريز) adj. Unfortunate.
s.m. Stalk, stock, trunk, stem; — a spider's web.
more: s. m. 1. A stalk, stock, trunk, stem. 2. A spiderʼs web.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنه.

ety: for A. طعنة, n. of un. fr. t̤aʻn, q.v.
def: s.m. Blame, reproach, chiding, taunting, taunt, reviling, ridicule, scoff, gibe, derision; ignominy, disgrace; aspersion
more: s. m. Taunting, reproach, gibe, scoff, ignominy, disgrace, aspersion. طعنه زن t̤aﻌna-zan, A reproacher, taunter, asperser, censurer, sneerer. طعنه زني t̤a na-zan-ī, s. f. Taunting, jeering, scoffing, reproaching. طعنه مارنا t̤aﻌna mārnā, To cast reproach, to reproach, gibe, twit.
subs: (corr. of t̤aʻna-tashnīʻ), s.m. pl. Taunts, scoffs, gibes, sneers, &c.
or t̤aʻne denā (-ko), To taunt, to ridicule, deride, &c.
s.m. A reproacher, reviler, taunter, asperser, scoffer, &c.
(-par), To jeer (at), scoff (at), to ridicule, deride, taunt, &c. ( = t̤aʻna denā)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طَعْنَه (ta'na), borrowed from Arabic طَعْنَة (ṭaʕna), from طَعَنَ (ṭaʕana). First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi طعنہ (t̤'nh /⁠ta'na⁠/).

def: s.m. corr. of طعنه taʻna, q.v. s.m. The threads that are extended lengthwise upon a loom, the warp
subs: v.n. To fidget, to dance attendance
(-ke), To set or dispose the warp (of a piece of cloth); to weave; — to wander hither and thither without profit; to plan and exert oneself to no purpose.

def: s.m. Pudendum muliebre; clitoris.

ety: inf. n. v of نعم 'to be in a state of ease,' &c.
def: s.m. Enjoying in abundance the conveniences and comforts of life; enjoyment, ease, happiness; prosperity.

ety: prob. corr. of A. اطناب at̤nāb, pl. of t̤unub, 'a tent rope'; rt. طنب
def: s.f. A tent-rope; a long rope used in measuring land.
more: n. f. A tent rope ( rassā ). arāzī ek-tanābī , n. f. Land which grows but one crop in the year; rice land. do-tanābī , n. f. See do-faslī .

ety: inf. n. v. of نبه 'to recollect,' &c.
def: s.m. f. (?), Advice, admonition; animadversion, &c. (see tambīh); — bashfulness, modesty.
more: (inf. v. of نبه) s. m. f. 1. Admonition, advice, animadversion, punishment. 2. Bashfulness, modesty.

ety: inf. n. vi of ند 'to flee'
def: s.m. Being dispersed, flying in disorder; dispersion, flight.
more: (inf. vi. of ندّ Heb. and Aram. Fled) s. Dispersion, flight.

ety: inf. n. v of ندم 'to repent'
def: s.m. Repentance, penitence; shame, bashfulness, modesty.
more: (inf. v. of ندم) s. m. Penitence; bashfulness, modesty.

ety: inf. n. v of نفر 'to run away in fright'
def: s.m. Avoiding, shunning; aversion, disgust.
more: (inf. v. of نفر) s. m. Aversion, disgust.

ety: inf. n. v of نفس; from nafas 'breath'
def: s.m. Breathing, respiration; heaving a deep sigh.
more: (inf. v. of نفس) s. m. Respiration, breathing.

ety: inf. n. vi of نفي 'to prohibit, to deny'
def: s.f. Expelling; pursuing, persecuting, seeking to ruin one another; denial (that another has fulfilled a contract or paid a debt, &c.).
more: (inf. vi. of نفي) s. f. 1. Expelling, pursuing, persecuting. 2. Denial (that another person has performed some thing, as that he has paid a debt).

ety: inf. n. vi of نفر 'to run away in fright'
def: s.m. Flying from one another; mutual repugnance or aversion; disputing or contending (for honour) before a judge.
more: (inf. vi. of نفر) s. m. Mutual repugnance or aversion, disputing for honour before a judge.

ety: inf. n. vi of نہي 'to reach, arrive'
def: s.f. Reaching to the end, arriving (at the end of a journey, &c.); being brought to a termination; finish, completion.
more: (inf. vi. of نـﮩ‍ي) s. f. Finishing, completing, arriving at the end or place of destination.

def: s.f. A harsh sound; jingle, tinkle, ring (of metal, china, &c.); throb, beat, shooting pain; spirit, mettle.

ety: inf. n. ii of تخنخ 'to remove,' &c.
def: s.m. Clearing the throat, hemming, hawking.
more: (inf. ii. of نخنخ) s. m. Clearing the throat, hemming, hawking.

def: s. (dakh.) 1. Solitariness, loneliness. 2. See دريغ dareg̠ẖ.

ety: inf. n. vi of نقل 'to transport,' &c.
def: s.m. A communicating together; mutual communication.

ety: inf. n. vi of نقض 'to break; to violate'
def: s.m. Being discordant or incompatible; contradiction; discrepancy; incompatibility; (in law) the advancing of two incompatible claims (to the same property).
v.t. To extend, stretch, pull, tighten ( = tānnā).
more: v. a. To extend, to stretch. See تاننا tānnā.

ety: S. तरूण, or तनु+Prk. प्पअं = त्पअं = त्वअं = S. त्व+कं
def: s.m. Youth; buoyancy or elasticity of youth; pride of youth.
despot; dictator; tyrant
wety: Compound of طَعْنَہ (ta'na, “taunting”) + شاہ (śāh, “king, ruler”). Coincidentially, also coined after Abu'l Hasan 'Tana Shah' the genial benevolent ruler of the 17th century Deccan kingdom of Golconda, wrongly portrayed as a vile and perverted despot in contemporary chronicles.

def: despotism, dictatorship
wety: Equivalent to تاناشاہ (tānāśāh, “despot, dictator”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: inf. n. v of نسل 'to bring forth'
def: s.m. Pedigree, genealogy. (The form is not met with in classical Arabic).
more: (inf. v. of نسل) s. m. Pedigree.

ety: inf. n. v of نصر; derived from naṣrān, 'a Christian'
def: s.m. Becoming a Christian (or Nazarene).
more: (inf. v. of نصر) s. The becoming a christian.

ety: inf. n. vi of نسب 'to mention the lineage' (of)
def: s.m. Resemblance; proportionateness, proportion; relation.
more: Ped. n. m. See نسبت Proportion. tanā'sub-i-āzā , Ped. n. m. Due proportion of the members of the body. nisbat tanāsub , n. m. ( Arith .) Ratio and proportion.
(inf. vi. of نسب) s. m. f. Resembling, relation, proportion.

def: s.m. pl. (of tanāsub), Proportions, relations.

ety: inf. n. vi of نسخ 'to transform'
def: s.m. Transformation, transmigration, metempsychosis.
more: n. m. نسخ transcribing. Transmigration; metempsychosis.
(inf. vi. of نسخ) s. m. Transmigration, metempsychosis.

ety: inf. n. vi of نسل 'to beget, bring forth'
def: s.m. Begetting; generation; uninterrupted descent through a series of generations
more: (inf. vi. of نسل) s. m. Uninterrupted descent through a series of generations.

def: adj. See s.v. tan.

ety: inf. n. vi of نول 'to give, present'
def: s.m. Taking meat and drink, eating
more: (inf. vi. of نول) s. m. Eating. تناول کرنا tanāwul karnā, To eat.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَنَاوُل (tanāwul), from Arabic تَنَاوُل (tanāwul).

def: s.m. A son; a male descendant.
s.f. A daughter; a female descendant
adj. Disputed, at issue, moot; — s.m. = tanāzǒʻ.

ety: inf. n. vi. of تزع 'to remove, to pull up,' &c.
def: s.m. Disputing, wrangling; dispute, contention, strife.
more: n. m. A. نزع See ان بن 1, 2, 3.
(inf. vi. of نزع or نزاع) s. m. Wrangling, strife.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَنَازُع (tanāzuʕ).

ety: inf. n. vi of نزل 'to descend'
def: s.m. Alighting; descent; decline, fall (of price, &c.), loss (of rank, &c.); leaping from (a horse, in order to fight), coming or rushing down into a plain against each other (two armies).
more: (inf. vi. of نزل) s. m. 1. Descent, decline (falling of price, losing rank, &c.). 2. Leaping from a horse in order to fight.

ety: inf. n. v of نزه 'to restrain oneself'
def: s.m. Being pure, keeping oneself free from vice or stain; studying continence; being modest or chaste.
more: (inf. v. of نزه) s. m. Purity from vice, &c. studying continence.

ety: inf. n. v of نزل 'to alight'
def: s.m. Descent, decline, diminution, wane, decay, falling off; degradation
more: n. m. A. نزل he descended. Fall; decline; wane; decay; falling off. tanazzul honā , v. n. To fall off; decline; to be degraded.
(inf. v. of نزل) s. m. Descent, declining slowly, diminution; condescending. تنزل کرنا tanazzul karnā, To descend.
subs: v.n. To fall off, decline; to be degraded, &c.

def: s.m. = next, q.v.
s.m. Copper
more: n. m. S. ताम, Pr. tambam . Copper. tāṅbe kā rupaiyā . A copper rupee; bad rupee; false coin. tāṅbṛī maṭṭī . Red soil. taṅbyā'nā , v. a. To be formed (copperas or verdegris), hence to have a bad taste (food).
s. m. (dakh.) Food (of a bird or beast of chase).
subs: s.m. A copper rupee, a bad rupee
s.m. Sulphate of copper, blue vitriol.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀁𑀩 (taṃba), from Sanskrit ताम्र (tāmrá).

def: s.m. The food of hawks and other birds of chase.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀁𑀩 (taṃba), from Sanskrit ताम्र (tāmrá).

def: adj. & s.m. Copper-coloured, dark red (e.g. tāṅbṛī maṭṭī, 'red earth'); — copper-colour (in pigeons, &c.); a copper-coloured gem of inferior value; a false stone resembling a ruby; — a bald head.
more: s. m. 1. A copper colour (in pigeons). 2. A false stone resembling a ruby. See تامڙا.

def: v. n. 1. To fly. 2. To hover about like a pigeon. Kyā ṭāṅchtā huā joṛā ātā hai . 3. ( Slang ). To take an airing; go out for a walk, etc. 4. ( Slang ). To eat up; gulp down. 5. To inveigle; wheedle; seduce. ṭāṅch k. yā lānā , ( Slang ). v. n. To quarrel; fight. Mujh se ṭāṅch lāë to sīdhā kar dūṅgā .
v.n. To move crookedly or tortuously; to fly round in a circle, to hover (as a bird); to practise tortuosity, or deceit, or wheedling, &c. (with), to cajole, wheedle, inveigle.

ety: ṭāṅć, q.v. +ṛā = Prk. अडओ = S. र+कः
def: adj. Cross-grained, perverse, froward, troublesome; moving tortuously to obtain an end; audacious.

s.f. (m.?), A scaffolding (in a field of corn, &c. on which a person keeps watch when the grain is ripe to frighten away birds and other animals), a raised seat, stage, platform; a projecting sheif (in a wall).
s.f. An ornament worn on the upper arm, a kind of armlet.
n. m. Dim. tānḍī , n. f. 1. A scaffolding in a field of corn on which a man or boy keeps watch to keep off beasts and birds when the grain is about to ripen. 2. A shelf projecting from a wall.
s.m. (local) = ṭānḍā, q.v.
more: s. (dakh.) Multitude, band, swarm, congregation, flock, drove ( Harris ).

def: s.m. A train or line (of cattle, &c.); a caravan (of merchants); the goods of a banjārā, q.v.; a venture (of goods); baggage, equipage; household furniture
more: s. m. 1. A venture (of goods). 2. Name of a place. 3. The goods of a banjārā.
subs: v.n. Baggage, &c. to be loaded or packed; to set out (on a journey); to depart (this life), to die.

def: n. m. 1. A family ( ṭābar , 3). 2. The goods of a banjārā , q. v.; baggage; equipage. 3. Household furniture. 4. A caravan. 5. A venture in merchandize. ṭāṅḍā ladnā , v. n. 1. To set out on a journey; to pack up bag and baggage. 2. To die; expire.
s.m. commonly ṭāṅḍā, q.v.
s.m. The commander or head of a ṭānḍā or body of men (as workmen, lascars, labourers, &c.).
s.m. Dancing (esp. with violent gesticulation); the frantic dance of the god Śiva and his votaries.
more: s. m. Dancing, especially with violent gesticulation. (Particularly applied to the frantick dance of the god Siva and his votaries).

def: s.m. See ṭaṇḍel.
s.m. corr. of tirendā, q.v. s.m. The float of a fishing line; a float; a buoy ( = taret).

Tando Adam (a city in Sanghar district, Sindh, Pakistan)
wety: From Sindhi ٽنڊو آدم (ṭanḍō ādam), named after Mir Adam Khan Mari + Baluchi ٹنڈو (ṭanḍō) (cantonment).

def: Tando Allahyar (a city in Tando Allahyar district, Sindh, Pakistan)
wety: From Sindhi ٽنڊوالهيار (ṭanḍōalahyār), named after Mir Allahyar Talpur + Baluchi ٹنڈو (ṭanḍō) (cantonment).

def: Tando Muhammad Khan (a city in Tando Muhammad Khan district, Sindh, Pakistan)
wety: From Sindhi ٽنڊو محمد خان (ṭanḍō muhammad xān), named after Mir Muhammad Khan Talpur Shahwani + Baluchi ٹنڈو (ṭanḍō) (cantonment).

def: s.f. Lassitude, weariness, exhaustion, syncope; sleepiness, sluggishness.
adj. Tired, wearied, overcome with fatigue or sleep, sleepy, sluggish.
s.f. = tandrā, q.v. s.f. Lassitude, weariness, exhaustion, syncope; sleepiness, sluggishness.
s.m. Grain (especially rice) after threshing and winnowing.
more: s. m. Rice (cleaned from the husk).

def: s.m.f. A plant the seeds of which are used as a vermifuge.
s.m. corr. of tannūr, q.v.
more: n. m. Cor. of P. tanūr . An oven. Jab bhūk lagī bhaṛve ko tandūr kī sūjhī, Aur peṭ bharā uskā to phir dūr kī sūjhī . Wom. Prov. By hunger pressed this wh—son's eyes are on the kitchen fire; His stomach filled, he's far away his eyes to feast elsewhere.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تُنْدُور (tundūr).

def: adj. See s.v. tan.
more: adj. Healthy; well; sound in body; hearty ( changā ). Jitne sakhun haiṅ sab meṅ yeh hi hai sukhan durust, Allāh ābrū se rakhe aur tan-durust ! Nazīr. Of all things in this world the chief and best— Honor and health be mine by Heaven's behest! tan-durust. k . v. a. To cure. See achchhā k . 3.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تندرست (tandurust). By surface analysis, تَن (tan, “body”) + دُرُسْت (durust, “good, healthy”).

def: adj. Well (in health).

n. f. Bodily health. 1. Tan durustī ko nipaṭ fazl-i-Ilāhī būjhye; Abrū se jag meṅ rahnā, bādshāī būjhye . Nazīr. Than health no Heavenly blessing deem so great, And honor in this world's a king's estate. 2. Ek tan-durustī, hazār nemat . Prov. Health is equal to a thousand other gifts. tan-dah , n. m. One who has great application. tan-dihī , n. f. Exertion; painstaking; attention; application; zeal. Tan-dihī se kām karo to hove. tan-dihī k.; tan denā , v. n. To exert or apply oneself; to take pains. Tan de to man le . Prov. Do with your might and hearts take quite. tan ko lagnā , v. n. 1. See ang lagnā (2, 3). 2. To affect one's circumstances. Jiske tan ko lagī ho woh jāne . He knows best whom it touches. tan kī tapat bujhānā , v. a. To appease hunger; quench thirst. tan-lagū , adj. lit . attached to the body. Devoted; zealous; active; painstaking (for another). Sab apne matlab ke haiṅ, mere tan-lagū to mar gae . Wom. Everybody for himself, my friends are dead. tan man. lit . Body and soul. Neh nagar kī rīt hai, tan man dīnoṅ khoë; Pīt ḍagar jab pag rakhā, honī hoë so hoë . Nazīr. Love rules the world—mind, body, and faith a sacrifice, Where love possession takes it nothing heeds nowise. tan man, dhan , Life and property. tan man se , adv. With all one's heart and soul. tan man mārnā , v. n. 1. To restrain one's appetites or desires; to suppress any feeling. 2. To concentrate one's powers or attention.
wety: From تَنْدُرُسْت (tandurust) + ـِی (-ī).

def: direct plural; oblique/vocative singular

ety: Pehl. tang; Zend tangista, rt. tać = S. तञ्च्
def: adj. Contracted, straitened, confined, strait, narrow, tight; wanting, scarce, scanty, stinted, barren; distressed, poor, badly off; distracted, troubled, vexed; dejected, sad, sick (at heart); — s.m. A horse-belt, girth; — a bag, sack; half a horse's, bullock's, or camel's load; — a bell
more: adj. 1. Tight; braced up; fast; close; straitened; contracted. 2. Wanting; scarce; scanty; narrow; short. 3. Badly off; poor; in difficulties; distressed. taṅg , n. m. A girth; a horse belt. Khetī, pātī, bīnatī, aur ghoṛe kā taṅg, Apne hāth saṅvārye jo lākhan hoṅ saṅg . Prov. Tillage and letters, a boon to beg, or saddle girth to strap, With your own hands do these though thousands at your elbows flap. taṅg ānā, ba-taṅg ānā, taṅg h . v. n. 1. To be hard pressed, hard pushed, pinched, in distress or difficulty. 2. To be harrassed, weary, sick unto death. taṅg-hāl , adv. 1. In narrow or straitened circumstances; reduced to extremity. Āj kal woh baṛā taṅg-hāl hai . 2. In one's last gasp; about to die. taṅg-chashm, taṅg-dil , adj. Miserly; niggardly.
n. f. A leg ( ṭāṅg ). ik-ṭaṅgā , One-legged; lame. du-ṭaṅgā , n. A biped.

adj. 1. Strait, tight, narrow, confined. 2. Wanting, scarce, scanty, barren. 3. Distressed, dejected, sad. s. m. 4. A horsebelt, a girth. 5. A bag, a sack, half a horseʼs, bullockʼs, or camelʼs load. تنگ آنا tang ānā, or تنگ آنا ba tang ānā, To be distressed, distracted or incommoded, to be exhausted, to be dejected. تنگ پوش tang-posh, adj. more: s. f. The leg. ٿانگ اٿهانا ṭāng uṭhānā, To copulate. ٿنگ دينا ṭāng denā, 1. To hang up, to hang. 2. ( lit. To give the leg) To be a catamite.

(-meṅ), To step in obtrusively, to interfere (in), intermeddle

(-kī), To seize or lay hold of the leg (a dog, &c.)
v.n. The legs to give way or fail; to have gout in the legs.

def: n. f. S. टङ्ग 1. A leg. 1. Kyā ṭāṅgeṅ ṭūṭ gaīṅ? Have you broken your legs? (Cant you step out?) 2. Tīn ṭāṅg kā ghoṛā . A lame horse. 3. Tāṅg brābar lauṅḍā, aur baṛoṅ ke sāmne boltā hai . This youngster scarcely three foot high, amongst his elders he his tongue must ply. 4. Khāë baṛyāṅ, aur ṭāṅgeṅ raheṅ khaṛyāṅ . Prov. Fed on pulse balls, set up on his legs. 5. Bāp kī ṭāṅg tale āī, aur māṅ kahlāī . Prov. The father's wife become she is a mother called. 2. A share; ( Brokers ) one quarter. ṭāṅg uṭhānā , Slang v. a. lit . To raise the leg. To copulate. Jaisī ek dafā ṭāṅg uṭhāī, vaisī sau dafa . Slang. ṭāṅg aṛānā , v. a. To intermeddle ( pāṅoṅ aṛānā ). Jāne na būjhe, harek kām meṅ ṭāṅg aṛā detā hai. ṭāṅg bal, lit . leg force. A trick in wrestling. ṭāng tale se nikalnā , v. n. 1. To pass under one's legs. 2. To funk; to be unequal to; to fail; forfeit. 3. To surrender; yield; submit; give way. ṭāṅgeṅ rah jānā , v. n. 1. To be tired walking. 2. To have gout in the leg. ṭāṅg lenā , v. n. To seize or bite the leg (as a dog). Āte hī ṭāṅg lī .
v. n. To be hung, to be suspended.
s.m. A small two-wheeled car (without covering, and on which one person only can sit), a 'tonga'
more: n. m. S. तग to go. A small two-wheeled open cart on which one person only can sit; a tonga.
s. m. A small two-wheeled car without covering, and on which one person only can sit. تانگاسوار tāngā-sawār, One who rides on a tāngā.
subs: s.m. One who rides on a tāṅgā.

more: s. m. 1. Two paisās: see ٿکا. 2. (dakh.) See غمزه g̠ẖamza.

def: adj. Having legs, legged (used chiefly in comp.); — s.m. A large reedy kind of grass, Saccharum procrum (it grows on high ground, and is usually as high as a man's legs, whence its name)
Tangail District: One of the thirteen districts in the Dhaka Division of Bangladesh
s.m. A hill pony.

n. m. A hill pony. ṭaṅgṛī , n. f. Dim. of ṭāṅg leg. ṭaṅgṛī par uṛānā , v. a. To throw one down with the leg-trick. ṭaṅgṛī-ṭūṭā , n. m. Wom. 1. One who is lame or crippled, or unable to work or earn a living. 2. A curse.
v.t. To cause to be hung up; to hang up ( = ṭāngnā); — ṭaṅgā-denā, v.t. intens. of and = ṭaṅgānā.

def: n. m. 1. A stitch. 2. A join; solder; cement. 1. Sone kī aṅgūṭhī, pītal kā ṭāṅkā; māṅ chhināl, pūt bāṅkā! Prov. A ring of gold patched up with brass; the mother a bad one, the son for a dandy must pass! 2. Dillī ke bāṅke, jinkī jūtī meṅ sau sau ṭāṅke . Prov. A Delhi blade, his shoes with a hundred patches overlaid! ṭāṅke udhaṛnā , v. n. 1. To be opened or unripped (the stitches). Ishq meṅ ādmī ke ṭāṅke udhaṛte haiṅ . Prov. Love rips open every joint of the body. 2. To be destroyed; ruined; undone. ṭāṅkā-pānā , n. m. Friendship; amity. ṭāṅke khulnā , v. n. lit . To be ripped open (the stitches). To be betrayed (secrets, designs). ṭāṅkā lagānā, yā denā , v. a. 1. ( ṭāṅkā bharnā ) To stitch; sew. 2. To join; solder.

So many thumps he has had, he is left with no hair on the crown of his head. ṭāṅṭ ganjī honā , v. n. 1. To become bald from carrying loads, a beating, etc. 2. To be ruined (by lavish expenditure).

Rām's name for aye dwell on, His name religion's essence is, Three worlds, seven regions up, seven down his Godhead all pervades. 3. Necessity; need; occasion.

def: s.m. Sound, din, clamour, hubbub, noise; fame; rumour; pomp, dignity, state; pride, vanity; imperiousness, domineering.
more: n. m. طن made a sound. 1. Show of authority; domineering; imperiousness. Ye tantanā apnī jurvā ko dikhāo, bandī nahīṅ uthāne kī! Mah. Wom. Go, show your rage to your wife, your humble servant (his mistress) wont endure it. 2. Childish passion; frivolous rage. Allah re tantanā! laṛkī to apne tehe meṅ āp jalī jātī hai . Mah. Wom. 3. Pride; vanity ( akaṛ , 7, 8). 4. Humours; airs. 1. Hākim hūṅ terī, hūṅ maiṅ Kalaṭṭar kī āshnā, Kyā tantanah dikhātī hai Tahsīldār kā? Rāhat. Your master I, for I'm the great Collector's friend, Cease then on Tahsīldār to plume yourself no end! 2. Chal, tere tantanah meṅ āg lagāūṅ! Mah. Wom.
s. m. 1. Sound, fame. 2. Rumour. 3. Pomp, dignity, state.

Leading or principal action of a ceremony, the regular order of ceremonies or rites, ritual; chief or essential part, main point; principal doctrine, rule, theory; a scientific work; a religious treatise teaching magical and mystic formularies for the worship of the deities or the attainment of superhuman power; a medicament, a drug, a chief remedy or charm considered as producing medicinal effects; charm, spell, incantation, enchantment; — demonstration, clear and right conclusion; the right way of doing anything; — raiment, vesture.

s. m. 1. Name of a religious treatise teaching peculiar and mystical forms and rites for the worship of the deities or the attainment of superhuman power. It is mostly in the shape of a dialogue between Siva and Durgā, who are the peculiar deities of the Tantrikas. 2. Charm, enchantment.

def: s.m. A musician; — one who follows a tantra; a practiser of enchantments.
adj. & s.m. Relating to the Tāntras; taught or contained in the Tāntras; following the Tāntras; — one who is completely versed in any science or system; a scholar; a philosopher.
s.m. Thread, cord, wire, string, line; warp of a web; cobweb; filament; a line of descendants; offspring, issue, race
s.m. = tāʼū, q.v. s.m. Father's elder brother, paternal uncle; — an elder, superior; — an astute fellow, a knave.
adj. Thin, slender, delicate, &c. ( = tanuk); — s.f. The body, &c. ( = tan, q.v.)
more: s. f. The body. See تن tan.
subs: s.m. A pore of the skin
adj. Having slender limbs, delicately formed, delicate, slim.

def: adj. & adv. Little, small, slight, thin; very little, the least; — a little, slightly; a little while
more: adj. S. तनुक A little. tanak, tanak sī , adv. Slightly; a short while.
n. f. ṭankā , n. m. 1. Tinkle; the sound emitted by China. 2. Throbbing (of the temples). 3. Spirit; mettle.

(or, tunak ), adj. Slight, weak, delicate, thin, effeminate. تنک حواس tunuk-ḥawāss, adj. Sensible. تنک حواسي tunuk-ḥawāss-ī, s. f. Sense, sensibility. تنک صبري tunuk-ṣabr-ī, s. f. Want of patience. تنک ظرف tunuk-z̤arf, adj. (a weak vessel) Tale-bearer, tatler, simpleton. تنک مايه tunuk-māya, Slight or weak in substance. تنک مزاج tunuk-mizāj, adj. Whimsical, captious, peevish, fretful. تنک مزاجي tunuk-mizāj-ī, s. f. Peevishness, captiousness.
adj. or adv. Slight, small, a little. adv. Slightly.

def: adj. Robust, corpulent.
more: (comp. of تن Body, and the particle مند) adj. Robust, corpulent.
wety: From Classical Persian تنومند (tanūmand).

ety: Pehl. tanūr; Zend tanūra: prob a Semitic word, connected with nār
def: s.m. An oven; a stove.
more: s. m. An oven, a stove.

def: s.m. Darkness before the eyes, fainting, a swoon.
more: n. m. A faint ( gash ); swoon.

def: s.m. Copper ( = tāṅbā, q.v.). s.m. Copper
subs: s.m. A copper rupee, a bad rupee
s.m. Sulphate of copper, blue vitriol.

def: s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts ( = tuwar).
s.f. A delicate or slender woman; — tanwī, s.f. Name of a plant; — (in Pros.) a kind of stanza of four lines with twenty-four syllables in each.

ety: inf. n. ii of نون not in use; formed from nūn, 'the letter ṉ'
def: s.f. Nunation, doubling the short vowels at the end of (Arabic) words in writing, and pronouncing them with the addition of the sound ṉ (e.g. خليفةً ḵẖalīfataṉ; تخميناً taḵẖmīnaṉ: see Grammar).
more: (inf. ii. of نون) s. f. Nūnation, doubling the vowel with the final letter of a word in writing (which in pronunciation gives such vowel the additional sound of ن ṉ; as, تخميناً taḵẖmīnaṉ, اتفاقاً ittifāḳaṉ ).

def: adv. See s.v. tanu. s.m. = tāʼū, q.v. s.m. Father's elder brother, paternal uncle; — an elder, superior; — an astute fellow, a knave.
s.f. A delicate or slender woman; — tanwī, s.f. Name of a plant; — (in Pros.) a kind of stanza of four lines with twenty-four syllables in each.

ety: inf. n. ii of نور 'to shine'
def: s.f. Illuminating, enlightening; illumination.
more: (inf. ii. of نور or نار) s. f. Illumination.

def: s.m. Mirth, joking, pleasantry; laughing (at), mocking, ridiculing; ridicule, derision, sneer, scoff, taunt, sarcasm
more: n. m. A taunt; sneer; sarcasm; biting speech ( bolī , 5). tanz'an , adv. 1. Tauntingly; sneeringly. 2. Sarcastically; ironically. tanz k. , v. a. To taunt ( bolī pheṅknā , 1).
s. f. 1. Mirth, joking, pleasantry. 2. Ridiculing, sneer, taunt.
subs: (-kī), To mock, jeer, laugh (at), ridicule, deride, &c.

def: adv. In joke; jeeringly, tauntingly, sneeringly, ironically, &c.
Tanzania (a country in East Africa)
s.f. See s.v. tan.
s.f. Keeping apart from all impurity; purifying, cleansing; purity, holiness.
more: (inf. ii. of نزه) s. f. Keeping apart from all impurity; purity, holiness.

ety: inf. n. ii of نزل 'to descend'
def: s.f. Causing (a traveller, &c.) to alight, receiving (one) hospitably; revelation; the Qorʼān.
more: (inf. ii. of نزل) s. f. 1. Revelation. 2. The ḳurʼān. 3. Causing (a traveller) to alight, receiving him hospitably.

ety: inf. n. ii of نظم 'to join, string,' &c.
def: Composing (verses); stringing or threading (pearls, &c.); ordering, arranging.
more: (inf. ii. of نظم) s. f. 1. Ordering, arranging. 2. Composing verses. 3. Threading pearls.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنظیم (tanzīm), from Arabic تَنْظِيم (tanẓīm).

def: organizational
wety: From تنظیم (tanzīm, “organisation”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: inf. n. ii of نظر 'to look' (at)
def: s.f. Beholding, contemplating; — likening, resembling.
more: (inf. ii. of نظر) s. f. 1. Likening, resembling. 2. Beholding, contemplating.

ety: for A. تنزية tanziyat, inf. n. ii of نزو 'to overpower'
def: s.m. Inspiring with desire; desire; propensity.
more: (inf. ii. of نزو or نزا) s. m. Inspiring with desire; desire, propensity.

def: Tao
wety: From the Wade-Giles romanization of Chinese 道 (dào, literally “The Way”).

def: Taoism

s.m. Heating (metals, &c.); heat, fusion; proof, trial, assay; passion, wrath, rage; strength, power; speed; splendour, dignity (these five meanings are peculiar to the Persian tāv, which, as regards the first three, is related to the Sanskrit tavās); — turning or twisting (metals, orig. by heating); contortion, twist, coil, curl, roll, fold; a sheet of paper
more: n. m. P. تاب S. ताप to heat. 1. The act of heating (metal); heat; fusion. Ek tāo kī kasar hai . 2. Passion; rage; anger; wrath. 3. Twist; coil; contortion. 4. A sheet of paper. tāo bhāo , adj. Very little. tāo denā , v. n. 1. To heat; melt; fuse. 2. To twist (string, moustache, etc.). tāo khānā , v. n. 1. To be burnt, overdone. 2. To be overheated (the blood). 3. To be inflamed, enraged, excited.

s. m. 1. Heat. 2. Passion, rage. 3. Strength, power. 4. Splendour, dignity. 5. Twist, coil, contortion. تاودينا tāʼo denā, 1. To twist. 2. To stroke the whiskers. 3. To heat (as, iron). تاوکهانا tāʼo khānā, 1. To be heated. 2. To be angry.
s. m. 1. A sheet of paper. 2. Proof, trial, essay. 3. Speed.
subs: s.m. Name of a powder or substance which, mixed with gold or silver, prevents the detection of adulteration
(-ko), To heat; to anneal; to melt, fuze; — to twist (cord, &c.), to curl or twist (the mustache, &c.)
v.n. To be heated, be made red hot; to be overdone, be scorched or burnt; to be overheated (as the blood); to be inflamed, be enraged.

def: adv. Rapidly, fast, quick.
more: adv. Fast; quick; rapidly. Nāk nāthe, kamar bande, raṅg wākā sāṅolā, Chāl chalte gābh de, chāl chale woh tāolā . Riddle. Ans. Charkhā . A spinning wheel. Her nose is bored, her waist is girt, her color is nut brown, As fast she moves all from her pregnant womb her issue's thrown. tāo'lā, tāvalyā , n. m. See utāvlā . tattā tāolā , adj. Hasty; precipitate.

ety: tāʼo˚ = Prk. ताव(इ), or तावे(इ) = S. तापय(ति), caus. of rt. तप्
def: v.t. = tānā, q.v. s.m. The threads that are extended lengthwise upon a loom, the warp
more: v. a. 1. To heat, to raise heat by blowing with bellows, to anneal. 2. To prove, to assay. 3. To twist.
subs: v.n. To fidget, to dance attendance
(-ke), To set or dispose the warp (of a piece of cloth); to weave; — to wander hither and thither without profit; to plan and exert oneself to no purpose.

def: s.m. Warmth, heat, burning; fever (com. tap); affliction, pain, sorrow, distress; passion
more: Rus. n. m. S. तप burn. 1. Warmth; heat. 2. Fever. Jo bar dekhe mujhe tāvan āve, soī bar mujhe biyāhan āve . Prov. Oh such a bridegroom's come to marry me! it puts me in a fever him only to see. tāp-tillī, tāb-tillī , n. f. An induration of the spleen attended with, or preceded by fever; splenitis .

n. f. 1. The hoof of a horse. 2. A tap; whack; hit; a sound of beating. 3. Pawing; the tramp of a horse's hoofs. Miyāṅ āyā kyoṅ-kar jānye, Ghoṛe kī ṭāp se paihchāṅye . Nursery rhyme. When young master comes home how are we to know, Click click his horse's hoofs will clattering go. 4. The penis , generally ṭappū or ṭāp-dār .
1. s. m. Warmth, heat. 2. s. f. Fever. تاپ تلي tāp-tillī, s. f. See تاب تلي under تاب tāb.
subs: s.f. Induration or enlargement of the spleen attended with or preceded by fever; splenitis, ague-cake ( = tāb-tillī)
s.m. Remover of sorrow or distress, an epithet of a deity.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit ताप (tāpá), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *tāpás, from Proto-Indo-European *tep-.

ety: Pehl. tap; Zend tafnu, rt. tap; S. तप, or ताप
def: s.f. Heat, warmth; fever; — tap-utarnā, v.n. Fever to go off
more: n. m. S. तप heat. Fever. tap utarnā , v. n. To go off (a fever). tap chaṛhnā , v. n. To come on (a fever).
n. m. 1. A cover; covering; hood. 2. The fly of a tent. 3. Spring; leap; bound.
1. ( तप ) s. f. Heat, warmth. 2. ( तपः ) s. m. Devout austerity, religious penance.

s. f. A fever. تپ اترنا tap utarnā, To go off (a fever). تپ چڙهنا tap chaṛhnā, To come on (a fever). تپ دق tap-i-diḳḳ, s. f. A hectick fever. تپ غب tap-i-g̠ẖibb, s. f. A tertian fever. تپ کا موت جانا tap kā mūt jānā, The breaking out of an eruption on the lips after a fever. تپ لرزه tap-i-larza, s. m. An ague. تپ محرق tap-i-muḥriḳ, s. m. An ardent or burning fever. تپ مواظبه tap-i-muwāz̤aba, A quotidian fever. تپ نوبت tap-i-naubat, s. f. An intermittent fever.
subs: s.f. Induration and enlargement of the spleen, &c. = tāp-tiliī, q.v.s.v. tāp
s.m. Breaking out on the lips after a fever, or of a fever in the system; the thrush
s.m. The breaking out of an eruption on the lips after a fever

def: s.m. Devout austerity, religious penance, &c. ( = tapas, q.v.); the hot season, the height of summer
subs: adj. Depending on the merit of penance
s.m. One who depends for salvation on the merit of his deeds of penance

def: v. n. To jump over.
v. a. To thrust oneʼs self into other peopleʼs concerns, to intermeddle, to interfere.
Ague ( Harris ).

s.f. Hoof (of a horse); stroke (of a horse's forefoot), pawing; sound (of a horse's hoof, or of beating, &c.), tramp (of a horse); tap, whack; patting, stroking, feeling (cf. thāp); — a bamboo frame for catching fish ( = ṭāpā)
more: s. f. 1. A stroke with the fore foot of a horse. 2. The sound of a horseʼs hoof in travelling. 3. A frame made of bamboo for catching fish.
subs: (or ṭāp-ā-ṭoʼiyā) karnā, v.n. To feel about (for), to grope, to rummage; to trifle; to find out and repair (leaks in a thatched roof)
v.n. To paw or beat the ground (a horse), &c. = ṭāpnā, q.v.

def: s.m. Covering, cover, the upper part or fly (of a tent); the hood (of a carriage, &c.); sound of dropping (as of rain, &c.); dropping, patter, drop (of rain, &c.); — spring, leap, bound
more: s. m. Spring, leap, bound, a drop of rain or other liquid. 2. A sound.
subs: ṭap-ṭap, adv. With a sound like that of a dropping liquid; drop-drop, patter-patter; in continuous drops, in a shower (as rain, tears, bullets, &c.); one after another, continuously, successively.

def: s.m. The hot season, a season of great heat ( = tap, q.v.); — a devotee, ascetic, &c. ( = tapasī, tapasvī, q.v.).
more: n. m. The hottest days in summer.
s. m. A worshipper, an ascetick, a devotee.

def: s.m. = tappā; ṭappā, q.v.

s.m. A bell-shaped covering of bamboo-work for placing over fowls; a hen-coop; a bamboo frame for catching fish ( = ṭāp); a framework covered with net or muslin placed over a cradle; a framework cover generally (with or without any external covering; and, locally, anything with such a cover, as a hut, a boat or raft, &c.; also a covering of cloth, &c.; cf. ṭap).
more: s. m. A hen-coop. ٿاپا ٿوٴي or ٿاپا ٿوٴيا کرنا ṭāpā-ṭoʼī or ṭāpā-ṭoʼiyā-karnā, 1. To trifle. 2. To grope, to rummage.

def: s.m. (local), An island in a river (cf. ṭāpū).

n. m. H. ṭapnā to cover. 1. A hen-coop ( khāṅchā ). 'Bol mere murge 'kukṛūṅ kū.' 'Jal gayā ṭāpā, rah gayā tū.' Double entendre . Boys' rhyme. Bravo young Cock, cry 'Cock-a-doodle-doo!' 'Burnt up your hen-coop's now, left only you!' 2. ( Auctioneers ) A crinoline. 3. A bamboo frame for catching fish. ṭāpā ṭoī k . v. a. 1. To grope for; rummage. 2. E. To repair the leaks (in a thatched roof).
s.m. = tapāk (the more com. form, q.v.).
more: s. m. Friendship, the anguish of love, solicitude of friendship, affection.

def: adj. & s.m. Heating, burning, inflaming; — fever, morbid heat.
s.f. Sound of dropping (of a liquid, &c.); dropping, dripping; drop; beat, pulsation, throbbing, throb, pain
more: (from ٿپکنا) s. f. 1. Pain, throbbing. 2. Sound made by dropping. ٿپک پڙنا ṭapak paṛnā, To drip. ٿپک نويس ṭapak-navīs, s. m. One who reports erroneously what he pretends to have overheard.
subs: s.m. One who reports erroneously what he pretends to have overheard.

def: s.m. Warmth, ardour, fervour, zeal; the anguish of love; solicitude of friendship; love, affection, friendship; apparent cordiality; — affliction, distress, uneasiness, disquietude; consternation; palpitation ( = tapak).
more: (Pers. تاپاک) s. m. 1. Affection, esteem, regard, love, zeal, ardour. 2. Consternation, affliction, uneasiness.

def: s.f. Throb, palpitation (cf. tapāk).
more: n. f. S. तप heat. Throb; palpitation.
n. f. 1. Drippings; dribbling; drops; percolation. 2. E. Throbbing; pain. ṭapak paṛnā , v. n. To drip; drop.

def: s.m. An officer formerly employed in revenue affairs.
more: n. m. 1. A musketeer; rifleman; gunner. 2. A revenue officer (formerly).

ety: tapak˚ = S. तप+कृ; or = thapak˚, q.v.
def: v.n. To throb, to palpitate.
more: v. n. 1. To drip; dribble; distil; trickle; ooze; drizzle; leak. 1. Pakke ām ke ṭapakne kā ḍar hai . Prov. When mangoes ripe have grown, their juices then are apt to run. (Full of years, death appears). 2. Ek ek roṭī, do do boṭī, survā ṭapaktā jāë, Are e logo, gilā na kījo, mere ghar kāj hai! Song. One whole cake each, two bits of meat, with gravy thin as may be, You cant complain, my gentle friends, to-day a feast I've made ye! 3. Ṭapkī paṛtī hai us se gadrāhaṭ, Hai ye ālam terī javānī kā . Wom. Raṅgīṅ. Her ripening bloom with juices bursting full, Hers is the wanton spring of days youthful. 4. Ām pake, mahuā gadrāne, ṭapak-ṭapak ras jāë, Pī bin mo pe rahiyo na jāë . Wom. The mangoes ripening are, the mauvas' juices burst and drop, My husband's absence tries me sore, I can no longer stop. 5. Nanadī chhavāve apnā baṅgalvā, Hamaro chhavaiyā pardesvā, Bundiyā ṭapake rī! Mag. song. My husba…
v. n. To throb, to palpitate. تپکنا پهوڙي کا tapaknā phoṛe kā, The throbbing of a phlegmon or bile, when coming to suppuration.

def: v.n. To drop (as a liquid, or as ripe fruit); to drip, dribble, distil, exude, leak; to beat, throb, palpitate; to ache, pain (cf. tapaknā)
more: v. n. 1. To drop when ripe. 2. To drip, to distil. 3. To throb, to palpitate.
subs: v.n. To drip; to drop off or down (as ripe fruit, &c.).

def: s.m. Heating, warming, burning, inflaming; distressing; the sun; name of a division of hell.

ety: imperf. part. of tapīdan, 'to tremble,' &c.; rt. Zend. tap = S. तप्
def: adj. Growing hot; becoming agitated; palpitating; trembling.
more: part. act. (of تپيدن) Palpitating, growing hot.

def: s.f. Heat, warmth, burning, glow, fervour; — s.m. The sun; the hot season; name of one of the divisions of hell; mental distress, grieving, pining.
more: n. f. S. टिप्पनी See پتري Annotation.
s. f. 1. Heat, glow, fervour. 2. The sun.

def: part. adj. = تپان tapān, q.v. s.m. Heating, warming, burning, inflaming; distressing; the sun; name of a division of hell.
more: part. act. (of طپيدن) Palpitating, flouncing, agitated.

ety: tapā˚ = Prk. तवावे(इ) = S. तप+आपय˚ caus. augment
def: v.t. To heat, to warm; to cause to glow; to test (metals) by means of fire; to make (a person) warm himself (at the fire or in the sunshine).
more: v. a. S. तप To heat (solids); to test (gold or silver) in the fire. Kharā, khoṭā tapāne se mālūm hotā hai . Prov. Saying. Good and bad the fire (or adversity) doth prove.
v. a. To pour a libation.
( तापन or caus. of تپنا) v. a. To heat, warm, make glow, make another person warm himself at the fire or in the sun.

ety: tap˚ = S. तर्पय˚ (caus. of rt. तृप्)+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To present a libation to (the gods or to the manes); to present an offering.
v.t. To cause to jump or leap over; to cause to bound, &c.
more: (caus. of ٿپنا) v. a. To cause to jump over, to cause to leap, to make to bound.
